title: Cochleanthes lueddemanniana
intro: Cochleanthes lueddemanniana, also known as the "Amazonian passionflower," is a flowering plant species native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, as well as being used to treat anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Cochleanthes lueddemanniana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of supplements and other phytomedicines.
taxonomy: Cochleanthes lueddemanniana is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subtribe Cymbidiinae and is native to Central and South America. Within the Orchidaceae family, it falls under the genus Cochleanthes. Further classification places it in the tribe Orchideae. Additionally, it is often included in the subfamily Epidendroideae.
